title: Quarto: a crash course
text: crash course Quarto — next-generation, multi-language successor R Markdown — guide applying department’s branding usedepartmenttheme package. Although aimed users usedepartmenttheme, hope broader interest, anyone welcome use adapt . three aims: show usedepartmenttheme document looks like rendered. explain install, use, customise themes. provide tutorial Quarto people know R new Quarto (coming R Markdown). new markdown, also hope convert : Quarto powerful way communicate pre-publication research findings colleagues collaborators, wrapping narrative around data code. already comfortable R Markdown Quarto, Applying theme section need get going. new , suggest reading order. don’t care usedepartmenttheme can skip Anatomy Quarto document. depth (including make PDFs, Word documents, books, websites, dashboards) Quarto guide excellent, R Markdown Cookbook, still applies. homage original R Markdown tutorial, examples use base R’s built-pressure cars datasets (McNeil 1977). questions, suggestions, like contribute, please open issue GitHub page. Quarto R Markdown? Quarto successor R Markdown. Documents use .qmd extension R Markdown knowledge carries straight , syntax refreshes noted throughout page (chunk options move #| comments, inline code gains braces, tabsets become fenced ::: {.panel-tabset} blocks, output: becomes format:). Unlike R Markdown, one Quarto install works across R, Python, Julia, ships command-line tool, depend single R package.

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: usedepartmenttheme installs branded Quarto HTML extension project single function call. .qmd files need format: key. Install package (installs pak first don’t ): project root (folder containing .qmd files), run installer department . Florey Institute: themes lshtm(), lshtm_mrcg() (MRC Unit Gambia), lshtm_mrcu() (MRC Unit Uganda). creates _extensions/ folder (commit version control collaborators can render without package installed) _quarto.yml. Add matching format: key document’s YAML (Console output provides ): Render (Render button RStudio, quarto render). get HTML file department’s branded header bar, logo, matching browser-tab favicon. Every theme accepts colour, logo, favicon: Use custom() base colour logo — package derives lighter/paler tints readable header-text colour automatically: Tip themes impose document defaults — options like toc, code_fold, self_contained opt-arguments every installer. See package README full list.
code: if (!require("pak", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("jackwgoodall/usedepartmenttheme") usedepartmenttheme::florey() --- title: "My analysis" format: florey-html --- usedepartmenttheme::florey(logo = "images/my-logo.png", colour = "#450099") usedepartmenttheme::custom(colour = "#003865", logo = "images/logo.png",                            name = "My Lab")

what: Overriding the colour, logo, or favicon
title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Every theme accepts colour, logo, favicon:
code: usedepartmenttheme::florey(logo = "images/my-logo.png", colour = "#450099")

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Use custom() base colour logo — package derives lighter/paler tints readable header-text colour automatically: Tip themes impose document defaults — options like toc, code_fold, self_contained opt-arguments every installer. See package README full list.
code: usedepartmenttheme::custom(colour = "#003865", logo = "images/logo.png",                            name = "My Lab")

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Quarto document made : YAML header, formatted text, code chunks. YAML header (“YAML Ain’t Markup Language” — recursive acronyms big coding circles) tells Quarto render document. essential entry format:; ’d suggest html almost tasks (though PDF Word uses). much header can make documents accessible, ’ll see. Formatted text makes Quarto good sharing pre-publication work: lets build narrative around data want present. options shown Formatting section. Code chunks sections R code either set things produce output show. Code inside chunk behaves much like normal R — plots console output appear directly chunk created .

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: executable code lives code chunks, framed ```{r} start ``` end. Insert one manually, Insert menu, Ctrl/Cmd + Alt + . can give chunk label r, makes easier find later (appears editor’s outline). chunk shows source (via echo: fenced option) followed result: Quarto, chunk options written inside chunk special #| (“hash-pipe”) comment lines, rather chunk header R Markdown. sit just opening ```{r}. example, lines label chunk hide code keeping output: Rather repeat options every chunk, set document-wide defaults YAML execute: block (see top file): document uses: code-fold: true YAML, code shown hidden behind Code toggle default — nice compromise collaborators want see code just want content. Click ▸ Code chunk expand . warning: false message: false, warnings messages hidden output. Watch ’re building document, won’t surface render. options(scipen = 999, digits = 2) (hidden setup chunk) switch scientific notation round two decimal places. Adjust taste. useful per-chunk options : per-chunk option always takes precedence document default. show every chunk except one, keep default add #| echo: false one chunk. ’ll heavy data processing, ’s also worth reading caching Quarto’s freeze feature. Packages used render must loaded document, just session. robust pattern bootstrap pacman let install load everything else: also set global ggplot2 theme , front, every plot inherits (discussed Plot themes):
code: ```{r} #| label: chunk-one 1 + 1 ``` [1] 2 #| label: chunk-two #| echo: false execute:   warning: false   message: false if (!require("pacman", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pacman") pacman::p_load(tidyverse, kableExtra) theme_set(   theme_minimal() +     theme(       panel.grid.major = element_blank(),        # remove major grid lines       panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),        # remove minor grid lines       panel.background = element_blank(),         # remove the background       axis.line        = element_line(colour = "black")  # solid axis line     ) )

Click ▸ Code chunk expand . warning: false message: false, warnings messages hidden output. Adjust taste. useful per-chunk options : per-chunk option always takes precedence document default. show every chunk except one, keep default add #| echo: false one chunk. ’ll heavy data processing, ’s also worth reading caching Quarto’s freeze feature.

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Packages used render must loaded document, just session. robust pattern bootstrap pacman let install load everything else: also set global ggplot2 theme , front, every plot inherits (discussed Plot themes):
code: if (!require("pacman", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pacman") pacman::p_load(tidyverse, kableExtra) theme_set(   theme_minimal() +     theme(       panel.grid.major = element_blank(),        # remove major grid lines       panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),        # remove minor grid lines       panel.background = element_blank(),         # remove the background       axis.line        = element_line(colour = "black")  # solid axis line     ) )

title: Quarto: a crash course
text:
Create headings increasing numbers hashes (#) — hashes, smaller heading. Anything level 4 (####) appears floating table contents (controlled toc-depth: YAML). heading 2 (##). heading 3 (###). heading 4 (####). Heading 5 (#####) still renders, won’t appear table contents given toc-depth: 4. Links made putting text square brackets followed URL round brackets, space : makes Link. Equations wrapped $ inline $$ displayed maths: ∑=1n(......equation)2 \sum_{= 1}^{n}{(......equation)^2} reference mathematical notation . Three asterisks make horizontal rule: heavier divide can made little HTML: (swap black another colour name hex code match style). Markdown largely ignores whitespace. Two spaces end line start new line; something explicit (multiple blank lines) insert
.   inserts non-breaking space. Tables can written hand (Text options ), elegant ones come piping data frame kableExtra: hover table Many options documented kableExtra site.
code: [Link](https://github.com/jackwgoodall/usedepartmenttheme/) <hr style="border: 1px solid black; width: 100%; margin: 1em 0;"> table(pressure$temperature[1:5], pressure$pressure[1:5]) |>   kbl(caption = "A hover table") |>   kable_paper("hover", full_width = FALSE)

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: can drop values R objects straight prose, statements update automatically data changes. Inline code Quarto written back ticks leading {r} marker: renders : median pressure pressure dataset 8.8. data changed, number update next render. (change R Markdown, marker just r, without braces.)
code: The median pressure is 8.8.

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Tabs one effective ways make data-rich document approachable. Quarto, wrap set -level headings fenced ::: {.panel-tabset} block; heading becomes tab: Standard Red dots Blue triangles    (R Markdown done {.tabset} attribute parent heading; fenced-div approach Quarto equivalent.)
code: p <- pressure |>   ggplot(aes(x = temperature, y = pressure)) +   geom_point() p p + geom_point(colour = "red", size = 4) p + geom_point(colour = "#093daf", size = 4, shape = 17)

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Set rendered size fig-width fig-height chunk options (inches): Figure 1 much page figure occupies set -width (-height): Figure 2 Quarto lays multiple outputs layout-ncol: Figure 3: Figure 1 Figure 4: Figure 2 (replaces R Markdown’s .width = "50%" + fig.show = "hold" recipe.) ordinary ggplot2 code can switch themes (theme_minimal() popular). called theme_set() top document, every plot inherits look unless specifies : Figure 5 Figure 6
code: p + geom_point(colour = "#093daf", size = 4, shape = 17) p + geom_point(colour = "#093daf", size = 4, shape = 17) p + geom_point(colour = "#093daf", size = 4, shape = 17) p + geom_point(colour = "#c21033", size = 4, shape = 17) p + geom_point(colour = "#c21033", size = 4, shape = 17) +   ggtitle("This…") +   theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 24))  p + geom_point(colour = "#c21033", size = 4, shape = 17) +   ggtitle("…rather than this") +   theme_grey() +   theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 24))

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Quarto’s callout blocks clean way flag notes, tips, warnings — feature R Markdown lacks. Write fenced divs: Note neutral note. Tip helpful tip. Warning Something careful .
code: ::: {.callout-warning} Mind the gap. :::

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Citations come BibTeX file referenced YAML (bibliography:). formatting style can changed Citation Style Language file: download one Zotero’s style repository, put project, point csl: . example, use Nature’s style: easiest way build .bib file reference manager Zotero — right-click one items export, File → Export Library whole thing. Online tools can also turn DOIs BibTeX. inline citation @ followed reference key (mcneil1977interactive ), renders McNeil (1977). set link-citations: true, hyperlinks bibliography. Adding # References heading end document renders bibliography automatically.
code: bibliography: references.bib csl: nature.csl link-citations: true

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Citations come BibTeX file referenced YAML (bibliography:). formatting style can changed Citation Style Language file: download one Zotero’s style repository, put project, point csl: . example, use Nature’s style:
code: bibliography: references.bib csl: nature.csl link-citations: true

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: Link another section putting text square brackets followed heading’s slug — lower case, punctuation, spaces hyphens: gives formatting section. Quarto also richer cross-referencing system (label section {#sec-foo} refer @sec-foo number-sections: true); see Quarto docs.
code: [the formatting section](#formatting)

title: Quarto: a crash course
text: appeared already; full header document note line. YAML space-sensitive — child lines indented parent. install usedepartmenttheme extension, ’d swap whole format: block single key format: florey-html, theme supplies styling, header, favicon .
code: title: "Quarto: a crash course"        # document title subtitle: "…"                          # optional subtitle author: "Jack Goodall"                 # your name date: last-modified                    # auto-fills the last render date date-format: "[Last rendered] DD-MM-YYYY"  # UK-style date bibliography: references.bib           # BibTeX source for citations link-citations: true                   # hyperlink citations to the bibliography format:   html:                                # render to HTML     toc: true                          # add a table of contents     toc-depth: 4                       # include headings down to level 4     code-fold: true                    # show code, but folded by default     code-tools: true                   # add the document code menu execute:   warning: false                       # hide warnings document-wide   message: false                       # hide messages document-wide

text: hope crash course useful. ’d love feedback — good bad — via issue package page.

what: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: quick easy way format Quarto documents academic department’s theme standard Quarto preferences. set applied simple tweak YAML. package currently four default themes build plus function supply logo colour scheme. department added package please either add pull request open issue GitHub page.

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: themes impose document defaults can opt settings want (see Optional settings ).

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: Much package (logo, brand colours, typography applied across project now supported natively Quarto _brand.yml file: Quarto branding: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/metadata/brand.html usedepartmenttheme exists streamline installable Quarto extension, collaborators can render single format: key per-project setup.

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
code: pak::pkg_install("jackwgoodall/usedepartmenttheme")

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: Run project root (directory .qmd files): installs _extensions/<dept>/ folder. commit version control collaborators can render output without package installed.
code: usedepartmenttheme::custom(colour = "#003865")   # any organisation usedepartmenttheme::lshtm() usedepartmenttheme::lshtm_mrcg() usedepartmenttheme::lshtm_mrcu() usedepartmenttheme::florey()

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
code: usedepartmenttheme::lshtm(logo = "images/logo.png") usedepartmenttheme::lshtm(colour = "#003865") usedepartmenttheme::florey(logo = "images/florey.png", overwrite = TRUE)

what: Not one of the departments? Use custom()
title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: Supply logo single base colour — package figures rest. Lighter paler tints derived colour, header text set white near-black depending dark colour , header stays readable whatever pick. colour must supplied correctly formatted HEX code.
code: # Installs _extensions/custom/, used as format: custom-html usedepartmenttheme::custom(colour = "#003865", logo = "images/logo.png")  # Give it a name: installs _extensions/my-lab/, used as format: my-lab-html usedepartmenttheme::custom(   colour = "#6E2585",   logo   = "images/my-lab.svg",   name   = "My Lab" )  # No logo? The header bar simply omits the image usedepartmenttheme::custom(colour = "#B45C1F")

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: themes don’t force Quarto document options . install function accepts following opt-arguments belwo. Pass value write extension, leave unset fall back Quarto’s default: can always set per-document .qmd YAML instead (see Per-document overrides ).
code: usedepartmenttheme::lshtm(   toc            = TRUE,   toc_depth      = 3,   code_fold      = TRUE,   self_contained = TRUE,   warning        = FALSE,   message        = FALSE )

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: set theme package prompt modify YAML.
code: --- title: "Resistance Profiles" subtitle: "MRC Gambia — 2024/25" format: lshtm-html        # or lshtm-mrcg-html / lshtm-mrcu-html / florey-html / <your-custom>-html ---

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
code: format:   lshtm-html:     toc-depth: 2     code-fold: false

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: logo embedded (base64 data URI) header bar document’s favicon, browser tab shows logo . Supply logo logo = theme, install logo skip .
code: your-project/ ├── analysis.qmd └── _extensions/     └── lshtm/               # or florey/, lshtm_mrcg/, lshtm_mrcu/, or your custom name         ├── _extension.yml         └── resources/             ├── theme.scss             ├── header.html             ├── favicon.html    # logo embedded as the browser-tab favicon             └── logo.png

title: Branded Quarto HTML Themes for Departments and Institutes
text: default logo doubles favicon. use dedicated favicon instead, pass favicon =: order precedence : favicon = argument, bundled favicon.png shipped department (one), logo. department without bundled favicon simply uses logo bundling one optional.
code: usedepartmenttheme::lshtm(favicon = "images/favicon.png") usedepartmenttheme::custom(colour = "#003865", logo = "logo.png",                            favicon = "favicon.png")

what: Install a custom-branded Quarto theme — custom
title: Install a custom-branded Quarto theme — custom
text: generic counterpart built-department themes (lshtm(), lshtm_mrcg(), lshtm_mrcu(), florey()). Supply logo single base colour package works rest:

title: Install a custom-branded Quarto theme — custom
code: custom(   colour,   logo = NULL,   favicon = NULL,   name = "custom",   title = NULL,   path = ".",   overwrite = FALSE,   toc = NULL,   toc_depth = NULL,   code_fold = NULL,   code_tools = NULL,   self_contained = NULL,   number_sections = NULL,   highlight_style = NULL,   warning = NULL,   message = NULL,   fig_width = NULL,   fig_height = NULL )

title: Install a custom-branded Quarto theme — custom
text: colour Primary brand colour hex string (e.g. "#003865" "#abc"). Lighter paler tints, readable header text colour, derived automatically. logo Path PNG, SVG, JPEG logo file. NULL, header bar rendered without logo. favicon Optional path dedicated favicon image. NULL, logo used favicon (none logo supplied). name Theme name. Used (sanitising lowercase letters, digits, hyphens) extension folder name format key, name = "Lab" installs _extensions/-lab/ used format: -lab-html. Defaults "custom". title Human-readable title recorded extension's _extension.yml. Defaults "<name> HTML Format". path Project root directory. Defaults current working directory. top-level folder project .qmd files subfolders can resolve extension. overwrite TRUE, replace existing _extensions/lshtm/ directory. Defaults FALSE. toc Show table contents (toc). Logical, NULL leave unset. toc_depth Deepest heading level shown table contents (toc-depth, e.g. 3). Requires toc = TRUE effect. code_fold Fold code blocks behind toggle (code-fold). Logical. code_tools Show document-level code menu (code-tools). Logical. self_contained Produce single self-contained HTML file assets inlined (sets embed-resources self-contained). Logical. number_sections Number section headings (number-sections). Logical. highlight_style Syntax-highlighting theme code (highlight-style, e.g. "github"). Single string. warning Show R warnings rendered output (execute: warning). Logical; set FALSE suppress. message Show R messages rendered output (execute: message). Logical; set FALSE suppress. fig_width Default figure width inches (execute: fig-width). Numeric. fig_height Default figure height inches (execute: fig-height). Numeric.

title: Install a custom-branded Quarto theme — custom
text: Invisibly returns path installed extension directory.

title: Install a custom-branded Quarto theme — custom
text: lighter paler tints colour derived automatically used sidebar backgrounds, heading accents, borders; header text set white near-black depending whether colour perceptually dark (WCAG relative luminance), header bar stays readable whatever colour pick; logo embedded base64 data URI renders correctly .qmd files anywhere project tree, doubles browser-tab favicon. extension installed _extensions/<name>/ path, _quarto.yml created path one already exist. Use format: <name>-html .qmd YAML.

title: Install a custom-branded Quarto theme — custom
text: theme imposes document defaults . format arguments unset (NULL) default written extension pass value; anything left unset omitted Quarto's default applies. can also set per-document .qmd YAML.

title: Install a custom-branded Quarto theme — custom
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Your organisation's logo and brand colour — the package derives the rest usedepartmenttheme::custom(colour = "#003865", logo = "images/logo.png")  # A named theme, opting in to a table of contents and self-contained output usedepartmenttheme::custom(   colour         = "#6E2585",   logo           = "images/my-lab.svg",   name           = "My Lab",   toc            = TRUE,   self_contained = TRUE )  # No logo: the header bar simply omits the image usedepartmenttheme::custom(colour = "#B45C1F") } # }

what: Install the Florey Institute Quarto theme — florey
title: Install the Florey Institute Quarto theme — florey
text: Copies _extensions/florey/ path. Use format: florey-html .qmd YAML.

title: Install the Florey Institute Quarto theme — florey
code: florey(   path = ".",   logo = NULL,   favicon = NULL,   colour = "#1a1a1a",   overwrite = FALSE,   toc = NULL,   toc_depth = NULL,   code_fold = NULL,   code_tools = NULL,   self_contained = NULL,   number_sections = NULL,   highlight_style = NULL,   warning = NULL,   message = NULL,   fig_width = NULL,   fig_height = NULL )

title: Install the Florey Institute Quarto theme — florey
text: path Project root directory. Defaults current working directory. top-level folder project .qmd files subfolders can resolve extension. logo Path PNG, SVG, JPEG logo file. NULL, bundled LSHTM placeholder logo used. favicon Optional path dedicated favicon image (PNG, SVG, ICO, ...). NULL, bundled favicon.png department used present, otherwise logo doubles favicon. colour Primary brand colour. Defaults near-black ("#1a1a1a"). overwrite TRUE, replace existing _extensions/lshtm/ directory. Defaults FALSE. toc Show table contents (toc). Logical, NULL leave unset. toc_depth Deepest heading level shown table contents (toc-depth, e.g. 3). Requires toc = TRUE effect. code_fold Fold code blocks behind toggle (code-fold). Logical. code_tools Show document-level code menu (code-tools). Logical. self_contained Produce single self-contained HTML file assets inlined (sets embed-resources self-contained). Logical. number_sections Number section headings (number-sections). Logical. highlight_style Syntax-highlighting theme code (highlight-style, e.g. "github"). Single string. warning Show R warnings rendered output (execute: warning). Logical; set FALSE suppress. message Show R messages rendered output (execute: message). Logical; set FALSE suppress. fig_width Default figure width inches (execute: fig-width). Numeric. fig_height Default figure height inches (execute: fig-height). Numeric.

title: Install the Florey Institute Quarto theme — florey
text: theme imposes document defaults . format arguments unset (NULL) default written extension pass value; anything left unset omitted Quarto's default applies. can also set per-document .qmd YAML.

title: Install the Florey Institute Quarto theme — florey
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ usedepartmenttheme::florey(path = "~/projects/my-analysis") } # }

what: Install the LSHTM Quarto theme — lshtm
title: Install the LSHTM Quarto theme — lshtm
text: Copies _extensions/lshtm/ path creates _quarto.yml one already exist (required .qmd files subfolders can find extension). Use format: lshtm-html .qmd YAML.

title: Install the LSHTM Quarto theme — lshtm
code: lshtm(   path = ".",   logo = NULL,   favicon = NULL,   colour = "#0D5257",   overwrite = FALSE,   toc = NULL,   toc_depth = NULL,   code_fold = NULL,   code_tools = NULL,   self_contained = NULL,   number_sections = NULL,   highlight_style = NULL,   warning = NULL,   message = NULL,   fig_width = NULL,   fig_height = NULL )

title: Install the LSHTM Quarto theme — lshtm
text: path Project root directory. Defaults current working directory. top-level folder project .qmd files subfolders can resolve extension. logo Path PNG, SVG, JPEG logo file. NULL, bundled LSHTM placeholder logo used. favicon Optional path dedicated favicon image (PNG, SVG, ICO, ...). NULL, bundled favicon.png department used present, otherwise logo doubles favicon. colour Primary brand colour hex string. Defaults LSHTM teal ("#0D5257"). Lighter paler tints derived automatically. overwrite TRUE, replace existing _extensions/lshtm/ directory. Defaults FALSE. toc Show table contents (toc). Logical, NULL leave unset. toc_depth Deepest heading level shown table contents (toc-depth, e.g. 3). Requires toc = TRUE effect. code_fold Fold code blocks behind toggle (code-fold). Logical. code_tools Show document-level code menu (code-tools). Logical. self_contained Produce single self-contained HTML file assets inlined (sets embed-resources self-contained). Logical. number_sections Number section headings (number-sections). Logical. highlight_style Syntax-highlighting theme code (highlight-style, e.g. "github"). Single string. warning Show R warnings rendered output (execute: warning). Logical; set FALSE suppress. message Show R messages rendered output (execute: message). Logical; set FALSE suppress. fig_width Default figure width inches (execute: fig-width). Numeric. fig_height Default figure height inches (execute: fig-height). Numeric.

title: Install the LSHTM Quarto theme — lshtm
text: Invisibly returns path installed extension directory.

title: Install the LSHTM Quarto theme — lshtm
text: theme imposes document defaults . format arguments unset (NULL) default written extension pass value; anything left unset omitted Quarto's default applies. can also set per-document .qmd YAML.

title: Install the LSHTM Quarto theme — lshtm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Install at the current working directory (must be your project root) usedepartmenttheme::lshtm()  # Explicit project root usedepartmenttheme::lshtm(path = "~/projects/my-analysis")  # Custom logo usedepartmenttheme::lshtm(path = "~/projects/my-analysis",                            logo = "images/logo.png")  # Opt in to a full-featured document (nothing is enabled unless you ask) usedepartmenttheme::lshtm(   toc            = TRUE,   toc_depth      = 3,   code_fold      = TRUE,   self_contained = TRUE,   warning        = FALSE,   message        = FALSE ) } # }

what: Install the LSHTM / MRC Gambia (joint-branded) Quarto theme — lshtm_mrcg

code: lshtm_mrcg(   path = ".",   logo = NULL,   favicon = NULL,   colour = "#0D5257",   overwrite = FALSE,   toc = NULL,   toc_depth = NULL,   code_fold = NULL,   code_tools = NULL,   self_contained = NULL,   number_sections = NULL,   highlight_style = NULL,   warning = NULL,   message = NULL,   fig_width = NULL,   fig_height = NULL )

title: Install the LSHTM / MRC Gambia (joint-branded) Quarto theme — lshtm_mrcg
text: path Project root directory. Defaults current working directory. top-level folder project .qmd files subfolders can resolve extension. logo Path PNG, SVG, JPEG logo file. NULL, bundled LSHTM placeholder logo used. favicon Optional path dedicated favicon image (PNG, SVG, ICO, ...). NULL, bundled favicon.png department used present, otherwise logo doubles favicon. colour Primary brand colour hex string. Defaults LSHTM teal ("#0D5257"). Lighter paler tints derived automatically. overwrite TRUE, replace existing _extensions/lshtm/ directory. Defaults FALSE. toc Show table contents (toc). Logical, NULL leave unset. toc_depth Deepest heading level shown table contents (toc-depth, e.g. 3). Requires toc = TRUE effect. code_fold Fold code blocks behind toggle (code-fold). Logical. code_tools Show document-level code menu (code-tools). Logical. self_contained Produce single self-contained HTML file assets inlined (sets embed-resources self-contained). Logical. number_sections Number section headings (number-sections). Logical. highlight_style Syntax-highlighting theme code (highlight-style, e.g. "github"). Single string. warning Show R warnings rendered output (execute: warning). Logical; set FALSE suppress. message Show R messages rendered output (execute: message). Logical; set FALSE suppress. fig_width Default figure width inches (execute: fig-width). Numeric. fig_height Default figure height inches (execute: fig-height). Numeric.

what: usedepartmenttheme: Quarto HTML themes for LSHTM departments — usedepartmenttheme-package
title: usedepartmenttheme: Quarto HTML themes for LSHTM departments — usedepartmenttheme-package
text: Installs branded Quarto HTML extension (format: dept-html) project one function call. Four department themes provided, plus generic option:

title: usedepartmenttheme: Quarto HTML themes for LSHTM departments — usedepartmenttheme-package
text: lshtm() — LSHTM teal (#0D5257), white header text lshtm_mrcg() — LSHTM / MRC Gambia joint branding, teal lshtm_mrcu() — LSHTM / MRC Uganda joint branding, teal florey() — Florey Institute black header, white logo custom() — bring logo base colour; tints readable header text derived automatically themes impose document defaults . Optional Quarto settings — table contents, code folding, self-contained output, warning/message suppression, — opt-arguments install function: pass value enable setting, leave unset fall back Quarto's default. primary colour logo can overridden theme, logo also used browser-tab favicon. Much can also achieved Quarto's branding support (_brand.yml); see https://quarto.org/docs/reference/metadata/brand.html. package packages opinionated department themes installable extension collaborators can render single format: key per-project setup.

title: usedepartmenttheme: Quarto HTML themes for LSHTM departments — usedepartmenttheme-package
text: Maintainer: Jack Goodall jack.goodall@lshtm.ac.uk (ORCID)

title: usedepartmenttheme 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Four built-department themes: lshtm(), lshtm_mrcg(), lshtm_mrcu(), florey(). Generic custom() theme: supply logo single base colour; lighter/paler tints derived automatically header text colour chosen readability colour’s luminance. Themes impose document defaults. Quarto settings (table contents, code folding, self-contained output, warning/message suppression, section numbering, figure sizes, highlight style) opt-arguments every install function; anything left unset falls back Quarto’s default. logo embedded browser-tab favicon well header bar. dedicated favicon can supplied favicon =; otherwise bundled per-department favicon.png used present, falling back logo. README notes Quarto’s native branding (_brand.yml) covers many needs.
